author: somnergy
content: Thanks <@427491045308235776> I will put my thoughts here soon

author: somnergy
content:
Basically four things
- Does anybody even fully check it inside zkVM?
- The pre-check would be very expensive
- Pre-check is a stronger guarantee from state_root_a -> state_root_a_plus_1
- A bad witness can also come with a bad block header (incorrect root), so is it worth it? Since post check wont match good blocks. A world with malicious block builders + malicious witness generators and a well-crafted transaction that can take advantage of the "skip validating read-only" would suffer.

More analysis is needed on all 4 of these points

author: kevaundray
content:
- Yes, checking that the state being read is correct is needed. I’m not aware of any guest programs that do not check this.

- Yes, you need to check MPT proofs for account and storage reads 

- Yep, this is what we are proving, ie that there is a correct state transition without any state

-  Not sure I understand, when any type of node (whether stateful or stateless) validates a block, they need to have a source of truth. A new block with mpt proofs is checked against this source of truth. This is usually just the block hash and state root of the previous block

author: jsign
content:
To extend more on the logic behind the necessity of the guest program validating the execution witness.

If this does not happen, a block builder can easily create a proof that 95% attestations levels. Indirectly, zkCL attestors are just trusting the block builder.

In a reality with single slot finality it basically means a wrong block can be finalized. In 3SF, just means if the next 2 blocks are from the same block builder or colluded ones, then wrong blocks can be easily finalized.
